calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Default And Default Judgment, For Leave To Oppose Petition, And For Leave To File Motion To Vacate Arbitration Award; Declarations Of Evelyn Kottle Simmons
Off Calendar Jul-16-2002 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Daniel Spitzer, Esq. 818/998-9700 for respondent EVELYN KOTTLE SIMMONS moving for relief from default and default judgment
Howard Hoffman, Esq. 916/368-6000 for Petitioner ROBERT E. RAVERCROFT opposing party
*Evidentiary hearing on the question of service proceeded as ordered by the Court.
Evelyn Kottle Simmons, sworn and examined on behalf of the moving party. The following exhibits were admitted in evidence:
Moving party's -- Exhibits 1, 2 and 3 -- photographs.
Opposing party's -- Exhibit A -- Declaration
Jeffrey Bourne was sworn and examined on behalf of the opposing party. The following exhibits were admitted in evidence:
Opposing party's -- Exhibit B -- Document -- original proof.
Opposing party's -- Exhibit C -- business card
There being no further witnesses, counsel argued the motion for relief from default and default judgment. Matter taken under submission. (JJM/as-x) Reporter: Eric L. Throne, CSR 7855

Daniel Spitzer, Esq. 818/990-9700 for respondent EVELYN KOTTLE SIMMONS moving for relief from default and default judgment
Howard Hoffman, Esq. 916/368-6000 for Petitioner ROBERT E. RAVENCROFT opposing party

Argued and Defendant EVELYN KOTTLE SIMMONS' Motion for Relief from Default and Default Judgment, For Leave to Oppose Petition, and for Leave to File Motion to Vacate Arbitration Award continued for an evidentiary hearing to prove or disprove service.
